artPrompt: The charged moment of a town-green election as a crowd of townsfolk press toward a cluster of blank-fronted voting booths in foreshortened perspective, hands raised, a ballot dropped through a slot mid-fall. Golden afternoon light floods the leafy ivory-green commons, picket fences and casserole tables ringing the throng, blank lawn placards bristling, fervent civic crescendo, rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: Recall Election Day
text:
Hold the recall election. The table votes. Each player casts one vote per creature they control, plus one vote per Voter (so Voters vote twice).
The player with the most votes is declared the loser of the election (yes, the loser — it's a recall) and sacrifices half the permanents they control, rounded up. In case of a tie, you cast the deciding ballot.
flavorText: Polls open at dawn. Polls close whenever Petunia says they close.

extras.frontrunnerPunish: most votes loses half their board
